refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refinement adds observability (telemetry reporting) and clarifies the scope boundary (load→travel→dump cycle phases). High safety criticality requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure cycle time across ≥100 representative cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Compute p95 statistic. Verify that telemetry reports cycle phase timestamps and total cycle duration. Confirm that safety-critical operations (e.g., emergency stop, load securing) are not bypassed to meet cycle time targets.
sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The Vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump phases with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-CT-002: The Teleoperation system shall minimize operator-induced latency and decision delays during remote operation.
FR-CT-003: The Safety Services shall enforce cycle time constraints that do not violate safety interlocks or emergency response requirements.
FR-CT-004: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle phase timestamps and total cycle duration via Telemetry interface.

candidate_dps: DP-CT-A: Optimize Vehicle hydraulic and mechanical systems for minimum phase transition times; implement predictive load sequencing; minimize travel path via route optimization.
DP-CT-B: Reduce teleoperation latency via low-latency network link and predictive operator assist; implement cycle time monitoring dashboard with real-time feedback.
DP-CT-C: Establish safety-aware cycle time budget allocation: reserve time for safety checks, emergency response, and load securing; enforce hard limits on non-safety-critical phases.
DP-CT-D: Integrate cycle time telemetry collection at Vehicle and Teleoperation layers; stream phase timestamps and cycle metrics to central monitoring system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-CT-Integrated: Combine DP-CT-A (Vehicle mechanical/hydraulic optimization), DP-CT-B (teleoperation latency reduction), DP-CT-C (safety-aware budget allocation), and DP-CT-D (telemetry integration) into a coordinated design that optimizes cycle time while maintaining safety criticality constraints. Cycle time target is achieved through parallel optimization of vehicle performance, operator interface responsiveness, and safety-compliant phase sequencing.

potential_sources: Vehicle phase timing (DP-CT-A) and teleoperation latency (DP-CT-B) are coupled via operator command-to-execution delay; mitigation: implement predictive assist and low-latency network link.
Safety budget allocation (DP-CT-C) and vehicle optimization (DP-CT-A) are coupled; mitigation: establish safety-first phase timing constraints that vehicle design must respect.
Telemetry collection (DP-CT-D) introduces measurement overhead; mitigation: use asynchronous, non-blocking telemetry writes.
coupling_check.notes: The integrated DP is not fully decoupled, but couplings are manageable via explicit interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) and safety-first design discipline. Recommend establishing cycle time budget allocation table (safety reserve, vehicle phase times, operator latency budget) as a design artifact.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic design envelope and site conditions (e.g., soil bearing, slope, load mass).
Assumption: Network link latency is ≤ acceptable threshold for teleoperation (typically 100–500 ms round-trip for heavy equipment).
Risk: If vehicle mechanical limits prevent achieving CT_target, cycle time KPI cannot be met; mitigation: conduct feasibility study early.

Risk: Safety interlocks (e.g., load securing verification, emergency stop response) may consume time budget; mitigation: design safety checks to run in parallel with non-critical phases where possible.
Risk: Operator skill and fatigue affect teleoperation latency; mitigation: implement predictive assist and cycle time feedback to reduce operator cognitive load.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-CT-Integrated by explicitly defining the cycle time budget allocation table: (1) Safety reserve (emergency stop, load securing checks), (2) Vehicle phase times (load, travel, dump), (3) Operator latency budget (command transmission, decision time). This table becomes a design constraint for all sub-systems and a verification artifact.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Cycle time KPI is a system-level property that emerges from the coordinated behavior of all three sub-systems. The NGHE Solution IM owns the cycle time budget allocation, cross-system timing synchronization, and end-to-end cycle time telemetry aggregation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution streams cycle phase timestamps (load_start, load_end, travel_start, travel_end, dump_start, dump_end), total cycle duration, and p95 cycle time statistics to external monitoring/analytics systems. Enables Site Manager and Maintenance to track KPI performance.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives cycle task requests (load location, dump location, load mass, priority) from Site Manager or autonomous planning system. Tasking includes cycle time constraints and safety requirements.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits SafetyAlert when cycle time optimization conflicts with safety constraints (e.g., emergency stop triggered, load securing check failed, operator latency exceeds threshold). Alerts inform Safety Manager and Tele-operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution relies on Network Link for teleoperation command/response and telemetry transmission. Network latency directly impacts cycle time; NGHE Solution monitors link quality and adjusts cycle time budget if latency degrades.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of cycle time KPI. It integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services and owns the cycle time budget allocation and cross-system synchronization. Minimal diffusion: cycle time logic is centralized in NGHE Solution orchestration layer.

Vehicle owns mechanical/hydraulic phase timing (DP-CT-A) but does not own teleoperation latency (DP-CT-B), safety budget allocation (DP-CT-C), or system-level telemetry aggregation (DP-CT-D). If cycle time logic is distributed to Vehicle, it will diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, violating single responsibility.

Teleoperation owns operator latency reduction (DP-CT-B) but does not own vehicle phase timing, safety budget, or system-level telemetry. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation would require it to coordinate with Vehicle and Safety Services, creating diffusion.

Safety Services owns safety-aware budget allocation (DP-CT-C) but does not own vehicle optimization, teleoperation latency, or telemetry aggregation. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services would diffuse responsibility across all sub-systems.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because: (1) Cycle time KPI is a system-level emergent property that requires coordinated optimization across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. (2) NGHE Solution owns the cycle time budget allocation table and cross-system timing synchronization logic. (3) NGHE Solution aggregates telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation and reports end-to-end cycle time metrics. (4) Assigning cycle time to any sub-system (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) would create diffusion and violate single responsibility.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god object' if it owns too many cross-cutting concerns. Mitigation: Define clear, minimal interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and delegate phase-specific logic to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Risk: Cycle time budget allocation table may be difficult to maintain if requirements change (e.g., new safety checks, vehicle upgrades). Mitigation: Establish cycle time budget as a living design artifact with version control and change management.

Risk: Network Link latency variability may cause cycle time to exceed p95 target. Mitigation: Implement adaptive cycle time budget that adjusts based on observed network latency; emit SafetyAlert if latency exceeds threshold.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define NGHE Solution IM with explicit sub-modules: (1) Cycle Time Orchestrator (owns budget allocation, phase sequencing, cross-system synchronization), (2) Telemetry Aggregator (collects phase timestamps from Vehicle and Teleoperation, computes p95 statistics), (3) Safety Coordinator (enforces safety constraints on cycle time optimization). This decomposition prevents NGHE Solution from becoming monolithic and clarifies ownership boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-CT (cycle time KPI with p95 ≤ CT_target, duty cycle, telemetry reporting) is directly addressed by DP-CT-Integrated (vehicle optimization, teleoperation latency reduction, safety-aware budget allocation, telemetry integration). DP is complete, feasible, and verifiable. No FR-DP mismatch detected.

diffusion_risks: DP-CT-A (vehicle optimization) is owned by Vehicle IM; DP-CT-B (teleoperation latency) is owned by Teleoperation IM; DP-CT-C (safety budget) is owned by Safety Services IM; DP-CT-D (telemetry) is owned by NGHE Solution IM. Diffusion is acceptable because each DP component is assigned to the IM that owns the corresponding technical domain.

NGHE Solution IM must define clear interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) to coordinate DP components across sub-systems. Recommend establishing interface contracts (message schemas, timing requirements, error handling) before detailed design.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM contains the cycle time orchestration logic (budget allocation, phase sequencing, cross-system synchronization) and telemetry aggregation. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services IMs contain phase-specific implementations. This allocation minimizes diffusion and respects single responsibility.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is verifiable via telemetry-based measurement: collect ≥100 cycles, compute p95 statistic, compare to CT_target. Verification requires instrumentation of Vehicle (phase timestamps), Teleoperation (operator latency), and Safety Services (safety check duration). Feasibility is high if telemetry interfaces are implemented.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution IM with clear sub-allocations to Vehicle (phase timing), Teleoperation (latency), Safety Services (safety budget), and NGHE Solution (orchestration and telemetry). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Establish cycle time budget allocation table as a design artifact: (1) Safety reserve (emergency stop, load securing checks), (2) Vehicle phase times (load, travel, dump), (3) Operator latency budget, (4) Network latency budget. This table becomes a constraint for all sub-systems and a verification reference.

Define interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link: specify message schemas, timing requirements, error handling, and quality-of-service (QoS) parameters. This ensures coordinated implementation across sub-systems.

Conduct feasibility study to verify that CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic design envelope and site conditions. If not achievable, escalate to stakeholders for CT_target revision.

Define adaptive cycle time budget mechanism: if observed network latency or operator latency exceeds budget, NGHE Solution shall emit SafetyAlert and adjust cycle time expectations. This prevents safety violations due to latency variability.

decision.justification: Refined FR-CT is well-defined, DP-CT-Integrated is feasible and addresses all FR requirements, and NGHE Solution IM is the appropriate ownership boundary. However, DP-CT-Integrated is not atomic; it comprises four coordinated sub-DPs (vehicle optimization, teleoperation latency reduction, safety budget allocation, telemetry integration) that must be decomposed into sub-FRs and sub-DPs at the next level. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level to detail each sub-DP and assign ownership to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services IMs.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR-CT and selected DP-CT-Integrated pass all gate reviews. However, the DP is composite and requires further decomposition to be implementable. The four sub-FRs (FR-CT-001 through FR-CT-004) are well-defined and ready for next-level decomposition. Proceeding with ZIGZAG_DOWN allows detailed design of each sub-system while maintaining traceability to the top-level cycle time KPI.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The Vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump phases with deterministic timing within design envelope. [Owned by Vehicle IM]
FR-CT-002: The Teleoperation system shall minimize operator-induced latency and decision delays during remote operation. [Owned by Teleoperation IM]
FR-CT-003: The Safety Services shall enforce cycle time constraints that do not violate safety interlocks or emergency response requirements. [Owned by Safety Services IM]
FR-CT-004: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle phase timestamps and total cycle duration via Telemetry interface. [Owned by NGHE Solution IM]

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic design envelope and site conditions.
Network link latency is ≤ acceptable threshold for teleoperation (typically 100–500 ms round-trip).
Operator skill and training are sufficient to achieve target latency; predictive assist can reduce operator cognitive load.
Safety interlocks (load securing, emergency stop) can be designed to run in parallel with non-critical phases, minimizing time budget impact.
Telemetry collection overhead is negligible (asynchronous, non-blocking writes).
NGHE Solution IM can be implemented as a lightweight orchestration layer that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services without becoming a performance bottleneck.
open_risks: Vehicle mechanical limits may prevent achieving CT_target; feasibility study required.
Network latency variability may cause p95 cycle time to exceed target; adaptive budget mechanism required.
Safety checks may consume significant time budget; parallel execution and optimization required.
Operator fatigue and skill variability may increase teleoperation latency; predictive assist and training required.
Telemetry aggregation and p95 computation may introduce latency; real-time processing required.
Cross-system synchronization (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) may be complex; interface contracts and testing required.
evidence_needed: Cycle time budget allocation table (safety reserve, vehicle phase times, operator latency budget, network latency budget).
Vehicle phase timing specifications (load, travel, dump) with mechanical/hydraulic design justification.
Teleoperation latency analysis (command transmission, operator decision time, response execution) with network link specifications.
Safety Services timing analysis (emergency stop response, load securing verification, safety check duration).
Telemetry interface specification (message schemas, phase timestamps, cycle duration, p95 computation).
Feasibility study confirming CT_target is achievable under agreed duty cycle and site conditions.
Network link quality-of-service (QoS) specification and latency monitoring mechanism.
Adaptive cycle time budget mechanism design (latency monitoring, budget adjustment, SafetyAlert triggering).
Test plan for cycle time KPI verification (≥100 cycles, p95 computation, site conditions).
